summary:
Type 1 diabetes impairs Achilles tendon structure due to collagen disruption and increased stiffness, leading to diminished ability to withstand mechanical load during physical activity like a 10-km run. As a result, patients may experience greater injury risks or suboptimal functional outcomes when engaging in prolonged aerobic exercise. 

Note: The summary should not include any external information beyond what was provided by Agent1’s original text.

Type 1 diabetes causes collagen fiber disorganization and increases Achilles tendon stiffness, reducing its capacity to handle mechanical stress during a 10-km run. This leads to poorer tendon function and heightened rupture risk under sustained loading from long-distance running.
